column1: Dataset Title: Russian Arctic Vegetation Archive: Tazovsky peninsula (T1) Vegetation Plots

Dataset Reference: Telyatnikov M.Yu., Khitun O.V., Czernyadjeva I.V., Kuzmina E.Yu. & Ermokhina K.A. (2021). A contribution to the syntaxonomic diversity of the Tazovsky Peninsula, Arctic Russia. Botanica Pacifica. A Journal of Plant Science and Conservation, 10(1). https://doi.org/0.17581/BP.2021.10106
Dataset Table: Modified Species Cover Data for Tazovsky peninsula (T1)

Dataset Notes: Percentage species cover scale is used for vascular plants and bryophytes. Braun-Blanquet cover-abundance scale is used for lichens: r (rare), + (common, but less than 1 percent), 1 (1 to 5 percent), 2 (6 to 25 percent), 3 (26 to 50 percent), 4 (51 to 75 percent), and 5 (76 to 100 percent). Taxa are listed in alphabetical order. Both the dataset author determinations and the current nomenclature according to the Panarctic Species List (PASL) are listed.
